text.title: Kibana Queries, Dashboards, and Alerting Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Queries that target ERROR-level events MUST use keyword fields (`level:`, `service:`) — never wildcard `message:*error*`.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Time-range and exact-match clauses MUST sit in `bool.filter` (cached), not `bool.must` (scored).
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: `xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey` MUST be set to a 32-char random value before any alerting connector is created.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Every critical service MUST have a count<1 alert over its expected heartbeat window.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Developer roles MUST be scoped to their service indices; full cluster access reserved for SREs and compliance auditors.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) ELK is not the viewer of record OR (b) dashboard has no maintenance horizon (<3 months).
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
